fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to build out a holiday/business-day calendar library — think of it like that date-utility thing we prototyped for the payroll team a while back, but much more complete and multi-country. The main pain points we're hearing: scheduling systems are breaking because nobody knows which dates are actual working days vs. public holidays, especially around Christmas/New Year when weekends shift things around. We need it to handle a bunch of countries (US, UK, France, Italy, Japan, Canada, NZ, Austria, Australia at minimum), and it should also support regional stuff like Canadian provinces or NZ districts because right now people are getting the wrong dates for BC vs Saskatchewan. Also need the ability to work with totally custom holiday lists for clients who have their own calendars. The date-shifting logic when a holiday lands on a weekend needs to follow proper substitution rules — similar to how we handled the observed-date logic in that other project. Business-day addition and counting should work correctly too. One more thing: the easter calculation needs to be baked in since a lot of the European holidays depend on it. Architecture-wise, please keep concerns properly separated — I/O and business logic should not be tangled together. Make it clean and extensible so we can bolt on new countries later without rewriting the core.

A couple extra details from the team’s questions. For the holiday list output, keep it exactly two lines: first line is `count=<n>` where n is the number of observed holiday dates, second line is `dates=<comma-separated YYYY-MM-DD list>` in ascending order. These are observed dates with substitutions already applied, not the raw holiday dates. Example format is `count=10\ndates=1999-01-01,1999-01-18,...\n`.

On the weekend shift behavior, the default rule is `next_monday`, and under that setup if a holiday falls on a Saturday, the observed date moves to the preceding Friday, and if it falls on a Sunday, the observed date moves to the following Monday. There is also a `saturday_before_sunday_after` rule where Saturday holidays move to Friday and Sunday holidays move to Monday. The `weekend_observed_date` feature exposes these rules directly, including feature10 with rules `next_monday` and `saturday_before_sunday_after`, with Saturday moves to preceding Friday, Sunday moves to following Monday under next_monday rule.

Also, when the calendar code is `CUSTOM`, we should not load any predefined country holidays at all. In that mode the caller sends a `holidays` array in the input, and each item has a `date` (YYYY-MM-DD string) and optionally a `name`. That custom calendar should treat only those dates, plus normal Saturday/Sunday weekends, as non-working days. This is specifically what we need for previous_working_day, next_working_day, and business_days_add.

For factory lookup, the `factory_resolution` feature is just checking that a two-letter country code resolves to a calendar and then returning the holiday count for the requested year. The output format there is exactly four lines: `country_code=<CC>\nresolved=true\nyear=<YYYY>\nholiday_count=<n>\n`. For reference, AU for 2017 yields holiday_count=7 and that is national holidays only, no state. AT for 2017 yields holiday_count=13.

Japan needs a little extra care. The JP calendar should include fixed holidays with Monday substitution when falling on Sunday, the 'Citizen's Holiday' (kokumin no kyujitsu) sandwich rule where a weekday between two public holidays also becomes a holiday, Vernal Equinox Day and Autumnal Equinox Day which are astronomically calculated and vary by year, and the Happy Monday rules for Coming-of-Age Day (second Monday of January), Marine Day (third Monday of July), Respect for the Aged Day (third Monday of September), and Health-Sports Day (second Monday of October). For 2025, 16 observed dates are expected.

One other behavioral point on working-day search defaults, since that came up. By default, if `same_day` is not specified, next_working_day does NOT return the input date itself, so the search starts AFTER the input date. For example, if 1999-11-25 (Thursday, Thanksgiving) is a holiday, the next working day returned is 1999-11-26. The `same_day=true` flag must be explicitly set to allow the input date to be returned as-is when it is already a working day with zero offset. Similarly, previous_working_day by default starts the search BEFORE the input date. For example, 2007-01-01 (New Year, Monday) results in 2006-12-29. When `same_day=true` is explicitly provided, the input date itself can be returned if it is a working day. Also, negative open_days values must be rejected with `error=argument_out_of_range`, `param=open_days`, and `value=<the supplied value>`.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: CalendarFactory.get_calendar(calendar_code: str, region: str | None = None) -> HolidayCalendar

trigger_keywords: how should holiday_info output be formatted
what fields appear in holiday information output
format for observed vs actual holiday records
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The holiday_info output starts with `count=<n>`, then one line per holiday sorted by observed date. Each line must include `holiday=<YYYY-MM-DD>` (actual date), `observed=<YYYY-MM-DD>` (observed date), and `public=<true|false>`. Include `name=<text>` when a name is present, and `regions=<pipe-separated-list>` when regional applicability is defined. Fields appear in this order on the same line separated by spaces.

trigger_keywords: how is Easter calculated
which Easter algorithm is used
does the system use Gregorian or Julian Easter
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The system uses the Gregorian (Western) Easter algorithm. For example: 2010-04-04, 2017-04-16. Easter Sunday is the basis for computing dependent holidays like Good Friday (Easter minus 2 days), Easter Monday (Easter plus 1 day), Ascension (Easter plus 39 days), and Whit Monday/Pentecost Monday (Easter plus 50 days).

trigger_keywords: how does GB calendar handle New Year on Sunday
why is GB 2006-01-02 a holiday and not 2006-01-01
what is the substitution rule for UK New Year
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In the GB calendar, when New Year's Day (January 1) falls on a Sunday, the observed holiday shifts to Monday January 2. So for 2006, January 1 was a Sunday and is NOT a public holiday; January 2 (Monday) IS the observed public holiday. This is confirmed in feature4 test cases: `is_public_holiday` for GB 2006-01-01 returns false, and for 2006-01-02 returns true.

trigger_keywords: what Canadian provinces are supported
which regions are available for Canada
how do provincial holidays differ in Canada
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Canada (CA) supports province codes including at minimum BC (British Columbia) and SK (Saskatchewan). BC has a Family Day on the second Monday of February, while SK has Family Day on the third Monday of February. Both provinces share the same set of national holidays (New Year, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, BC Day/Civic Holiday, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Remembrance Day, Boxing Day) but differ in their regional observances.

trigger_keywords: what NZ districts are supported
how do New Zealand regional holidays work
Wellington district holidays New Zealand
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: New Zealand (NZ) supports district-level regional holidays via the `district` parameter. The WELLINGTON district includes all national NZ holidays plus the Wellington Anniversary Day (typically the Monday nearest January 22, observed as 2022-01-24 in 2022) and Wellington provincial anniversary. The NZ national holidays include New Year observed substitutions, Waitangi Day, Good Friday, Easter Monday, ANZAC Day, Queen's/King's Birthday, Labour Day, and Christmas/Boxing Day with substitutions applied.

trigger_keywords: how does business_days_add work
is the start date included when adding business days
how are business days counted in addition
hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
business_days_add counts business days EXCLUSIVELY from the start date — the start date itself is not counted. Starting from 2024-12-24 and adding 5 business days with Christmas (2024-12-25) and New Year (2025-01-01) as holidays yields 2025-01-02. The output format is three lines: `date=<input>
business_days=<n>
result=<YYYY-MM-DD>
`.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

context_pointers.vague_reference: that date-utility thing we prototyped for the payroll team a while back
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The business_days_add and business_days_count features (feature8) described in feature8_business_day_arithmetic.json, which perform exclusive-start-date business day addition and inclusive range counting

context_pointers.vague_reference: the compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The CalendarFactory / factory_resolution pattern (feature11) in feature11_country_code_resolution.json that resolves two-letter ISO country codes to concrete HolidayCalendar implementations via a registry or factory, enabling OCP-compliant extension without modifying the core engine

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect documented yet. Please check the test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the expected input/output contract, and refer to the PRD in start.md for the architectural constraints. If the behavior is still unclear after reviewing those, make a reasonable assumption consistent with standard business-day calendar conventions and document it.

feature10_weekend_observed_dates.json.description: Weekend-observed date adjustment rules move weekend holidays according to the requested observation policy.

feature11_country_code_resolution.json.description: Country-code resolution creates a holiday calendar for supported two-letter codes and reports that the code can produce a holiday list.

feature1_annual_holiday_lists.json.description: Annual calendar generation returns the complete sorted observed holiday date list for a supported calendar and year.

feature2_regional_holiday_lists.json.description: Regional calendar generation applies the selected province, state, district, or region and returns sorted observed holiday dates for that jurisdiction.

feature3_holiday_information.json.description: Holiday information returns each holiday with actual holiday date, observed date, optional name, public flag, and optional regional applicability.

count=11
holiday=2023-01-01 observed=2023-01-02 name=New Year public=true
holiday=2023-01-16 observed=2023-01-16 name=Martin Luther King Day public=true
holiday=2023-02-20 observed=2023-02-20 name=President's Day public=true
holiday=2023-05-29 observed=2023-05-29 name=Memorial Day public=true
holiday=2023-06-19 observed=2023-06-19 name=Juneteenth public=true
holiday=2023-07-04 observed=2023-07-04 name=Independence Day public=true
holiday=2023-09-04 observed=2023-09-04 name=Labor Day public=true
holiday=2023-10-09 observed=2023-10-09 name=Columbus Day public=true

feature4_public_holiday_checks.json.description: Single-date holiday checks report whether a date is an observed public holiday in the requested calendar.

feature5_working_day_checks.json.description: Working-day checks report whether a date is Monday through Friday and not an observed public holiday for the selected calendar.

feature6_next_working_day.json.description: Forward working-day calculation returns the first acceptable working date on or after the input unless same-day matching is disabled, optionally after adding open days and skipping supplied holidays.

feature7_previous_working_day.json.description: Backward working-day calculation returns the first acceptable working date on or before the input unless same-day matching is disabled, optionally after subtracting open days and skipping supplied holidays.

feature8_business_day_arithmetic.json.description: Business-day arithmetic adds business days exclusively from a start date and counts business days inclusively between two dates while excluding weekends and configured holidays.

feature9_easter_dates.json.description: Gregorian Easter calculation returns Easter Sunday for the requested year.
